rulings.Case Title: HARTFORD ACCIDENT & INDEMNITY COMPANY VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 12, 2007, LINE 9. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: HEARING AND SUPPLEMENT BRIEFING REQUIRED ON TWO ISSUES: 1.) DOES LABOR CODE SECTION 3860 REQUIRE THAT HARTFORD BE NOTIFIED PRIOR TO A SETTLEMENT HAVING BEEN REACHED OR PRIOR TO THE DISMISSAL OF THE ACTION? WHAT ARE THE UNDISPUTED FACTS AS TO WHEN NOTIFICATION WAS RECEIVED? 2.) THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH ELEVEN, ALLEGES THAT NASH AND P.G.& E. ENTERED INTO A NON-SEGREGATED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. WHY ISN'T HARTFORD BOUND BY THAT ALLEGATION? =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. ERIC AU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Stay Of Court Proceedings As To The 4th, 5th And 6th Causes Of Action; Petition For Arbitration Of The 4th 5th And 6th Causes Of Action In The 1st Amended Complaint.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 12, 2007, LINE 14. DEFENDANT EDWIN LAW AND LAURENCE LEE'S Motion For Stay Of Court Proceedings As To The 4th, 5th And 6th Causes Of Action; Petition For Arbitration Of The 4th 5th And 6th Causes Of Action In The FIRST Amended Complaint IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS ARE SEEKING TO ARBITRATE ONLY A PORTION OF THE DISPUTE. THERE IS A POSSIBILITY OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS UNDER C.C.P SECTION 1281.2(C) AND THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT PERTAINS TO FRAUDULENT TRANSFERS WHICH ARE NOT SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: THE UPPER NOE ENVIRONMENTAL COALITION AN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ITS PLANNING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner The Upper Noe Environmental Coalition'S Hearing On Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 12, 2007, LINE 19. PETITIONER THE UPPER NOE ENVIRONMENTAL COALITION'S HEARING ON PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. IT APPEARS UNLIKELY THAT PETITIONER WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE CLAIM APPEARS TO BE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND THAT THE APPROVED DOG RUN DOES NOT REQUIRE C.E.Q.A. REVIEW. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE IRREPARABLE HARM DUE TO INCREASED NOISE AND/OR HARM TO THE ENVIRONMENT. MOREOVER IF PETITIONER WERE ULTIMATELY TO PREVAIL, THE CITY COULD BE ORDERED TO REMOVE THE DOG RUN. =(302/PJM/AP)
